Empires & Puzzles Mistweaver Review: The Jungle Hunter's Orangutan Brawler

Mistweaver brings a unique anti-healing mechanic to the table, punishing enemies for trying to recover. But in the current era of massive stats, does this fast mana brawler still pack enough of a punch? We break down her skills, stats, and best uses.
Mistweaver Review: Stop The Healing, Start The Punching
Mistweaver: The Orangutan Brawler Who Hates Medicine

Mistweaver is a 5-star Legendary Nature (Green) hero from the Jungle Hunter family, originally released in March 2023. She burst onto the scene as a Fast mana "Hit-3" damage dealer with a very specific grudge against healers. In an era where keeping your team alive is half the battle, Mistweaver’s main gimmick is ensuring the enemy team stays hurt.

While she doesn't have a costume to update her wardrobe or stats, her base kit offers a mechanically interesting mix of damage, buffs, and a punishing ailment that turns enemy healing into enemy damage. She’s the kind of hero you bring when you see a heavy healing defense and want to watch them panic.

Visually, Mistweaver is a striking female Orangutan warrior, deck out in tribal armor with a vaguely samurai aesthetic—complete with a horned helmet and spiked gauntlets. It's a refreshing change from the endless parade of elves, humans, and goblins. The art captures a sense of calm lethality; she looks like she’s about to calmly explain why your face is about to meet her fist. The vibrant greens and oranges make her pop on the card, fitting perfectly with the Jungle Hunter theme. It’s definitely one of the more unique character designs in the game—monkeys with knuckledusters are always a win in our book.

Looking at her base stats (885 Attack, 881 Defense, 1524 Health), Mistweaver shows her age. In 2023, these were solid numbers. In the current meta of 2026, where new heroes routinely break 1200+ in base stats and power levels soar over 1300, she falls into the "average to low" category.

She isn't made of paper, but she certainly isn't the stat-monster she used to be. Her Attack stat is still respectable enough to do damage, especially with emblems and Limit Breaks, but she relies heavily on her passives and stacks to compete with modern damage dealers. She’s no longer a stat-stick; she’s a utility pick.
"Misty Punches" is a multi-layered skill that does three things at Fast speed:
Deals 350% damage to the target and nearby enemies. This is a solid "Hit-3" strike. It's efficient for softening up the center or finishing off a flank.
This is the crown jewel. It prevents the target and nearby enemies from receiving healing for 4 turns. Crucially, if they
to heal (e.g., a healer fires, or a passive healing effect triggers), they take 220 damage instead. This effectively turns enemy healers into liabilities.
She gains +15% attack against the weaker element (Ice/Blue), stacking up to 10 times.
This skill essentially says "No" to sustain strategies. The 220 damage punishment is fixed, meaning it ignores defense, which is excellent for chipping away at high-defense tanks.
Let's break down the math behind the monkey.
Fast. She needs 8 tiles to charge. With a level 23 Mana Troop (or equivalent bonuses), this drops to 7 tiles, making her very snappy.
350% is a decent multiplier for a Hit-3 at Fast speed.
Base Attack (885) x 3.5 = 3097 raw damage value.
This is before troops or defense reduction.

220 damage per resisted heal. If a HoT (Heal over Time) is active or a healer fires, this damage triggers. Against a heavy healing team, this can rack up 500-1000 extra damage over the duration without you doing anything.
Adds 150 damage over 3 turns (50 per turn). It's small, but it refreshes and grows if she fires again.
Adds +20% Attack for 3 turns. This applies
the cast for subsequent hits or tile damage, but helps keep her relevant.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Jungle Hunter family bonus provides a Health boost (up to +20% for 5 heroes). While nice for survivability, it's rarely a game-changer since fielding a full team of Jungle Hunters is difficult and usually suboptimal. The bonus during "Monster Island" is massive but incredibly niche.
Applies a bleeding effect. It starts small (150 total) but scales up if refreshed. Since she is Fast, she has a good chance of refreshing this to keep the pressure on.

Grants +20% attack for 3 turns on cast. This is a separate buff layer, meaning it stacks with normal attack buffs (like Boldtusk or cRigard). This significantly boosts her tile damage immediately after firing.
At the start of the battle, defense is increased by 20% for 6 turns. This helps mitigate her aging stats, ensuring she survives the opening volleys of a match long enough to fire her skill.
Upon release, Mistweaver was a strong contender. The combination of Fast speed, decent hit-3 damage, and the unique healing punishment made her a nuisance in wars and a solid flank in defense.

The game has evolved rapidly. "Hit-3" snipers now often deal 450%+ damage or come with even more debilitating effects. Pure healing block is still useful, but there are many cleansers and ailment blockers (Monks, Clerics, ailment immunity buffs) that can neutralize her primary gimmick. Her stats, while not terrible, mean she hits significantly softer than a modern 2026 hero.

. She is not a top-tier defender anymore. However, on offense, she remains a potent counter-pick against heavy healer teams or specific annoying tanks that rely on regeneration. She is still very usable in war depth teams and tournaments, particularly Bloody Battle (where the healing block is moot, but the damage is good) or Rush Attack.

Mistweaver works best in a "Green Stack" (3-2 or 4-1 formation) designed to kill the enemy before they can stabilize.
Drop the elemental defense and then have Mistweaver slam them. The fast speed synergy works well here.
Mistweaver blocks healing, silencer stops the cleanser. You control the board completely.

Do not bring her if you need a defense down hero and only have one spot left; she is pure damage/utility, not a debuffer in the traditional sense.

If you pulled Mistweaver early in your career, she is a fantastic asset. A fast 5-star who hits three targets will carry you through map stages and Gold/Platinum raids easily. Prioritize leveling her as your main Green damage dealer.

Mistweaver is likely a depth hero for you. She sits on the bench until you see a war team with two healers or a troublesome healing tank. She is probably not worth Limit Breaking (LB2) with Alpha Aethers unless you have a surplus or a specific love for the Jungle Hunter family. Her stats just don't justify the premium cost compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
The community reaction to Mistweaver was generally positive but mixed regarding her longevity.
“Powerful hit 3 nature. But I really love the resist healing feature.”
- Many players valued the utility over raw damage.
“Green hit 3 is a little stale these days.”
- Even at release, players felt the "Fast Hit-3 Green" archetype was crowded (Elkanen, etc.).
“She is a female orangutan apparently, according to the German description.”
- A fun piece of trivia for the lore hunters!
One player noted her potential against Cupido, using the healing block to punish his healing gift, though it requires specific timing.

Mistweaver is a solid, workmanlike hero. She doesn't have the flashiest lights or the biggest numbers anymore, but she does a specific job—punishing healers—very well. She’s the bouncer at the club who kicks out the doctor. If you need green depth or hate fighting against overheal teams, she’s your girl (orangutan). Just don't expect her to solo entire teams in the modern era.
Yes, for war depth and specific counter-play. No, for primary defense teams.

A detailed review of Mistweaver, the 5-star Nature hero from the Jungle Hunter family. We analyze her stats, special skill, healing block mechanics, and viability in the current Empires & Puzzles meta.
